chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH AT KARACHI CP D 580 of 2025 ________________________________________________________________ Date Order with signature(s) of Judge(s) 1. For orders on CMA No.4140/2025. 2. For hearing of main case. 25.02.2025 Mr. Ramiz Naseem, advocate for the petitioners. Kazi Abdul Hameed Siddiqui, Deputy Attorney General. Inspector Ahmed Rasheed Qureshi, Special Branch, Foreign Registration Office, Karachi. The comments filed on behalf of respondents 2 and 4 are taken on record, which states as follows: “2. Not concerned, while it is necessary for them to obtain Travel Permit from Bangladesh High Commission for travel back to their native country Bangladesh. 7. It is in-correct the allegations of harassment and demanding gratification money is false & baseless, the petitioners never visited to FRO Intelligence Special Branch office for getting NOC. 8. It is in-correct the petitioners never visited in FRO office for getting NOC therefore the allegations of harassment / threatened and lodging FIR against them are baseless. 9. It is in-correct, the reply of this para is already mentioned in Para No.08. 10. Correct, the Council of petitioners sent a letter on dated 30-01-2025 and requested for issuance their travel permits, while this office replied that they are staying illegally in Pakistan, therefore they can’t go back to their native country Bangladesh without court orders and without fulfilling codal / legal formalities. 13. Correct, in this regard the Honorable Court has passed the order that “Petitioners would submit all the relevant documents for verification and fulfill all the formalities as raised by the respondents, this petition is, therefore disposed off with the directions to the respondents that as and when the documents are submitted by the petitioners, the same shall be scrutinized as per law within the shortest possible time and the petitioners be permitted to travel to Bangladesh.” Learned counsel for the petitioners submits that they have no cavil to the aforesaid and seeks that the petition be disposed of as aforesaid.

Correct, the Council of petitioners sent a letter on dated 30-01-2025 and requested for issuance their travel permits, while this office replied that they are staying illegally in Pakistan, therefore they can’t go back to their native country Bangladesh without court orders and without fulfilling codal / legal formalities. 13. Correct, in this regard the Honorable Court has passed the order that “Petitioners would submit all the relevant documents for verification and fulfill all the formalities as raised by the respondents, this petition is, therefore disposed off with the directions to the respondents that as and when the documents are submitted by the petitioners, the same shall be scrutinized as per law within the shortest possible time and the petitioners be permitted to travel to Bangladesh.” Learned counsel for the petitioners submits that they have no cavil to the aforesaid and seeks that the petition be disposed of as aforesaid. Order accordingly. Judge Judge Khuhro/PA
